waypoints.narration: Often called the first man of jazz, Bolden's powerful cornet rang through New Orleans at the turn of the century.
Armstrong transformed jazz from ensemble music into a soloist's art, his trumpet and voice reaching every corner of the globe.
Ellington brought jazz to the concert hall, composing thousands of pieces that bridged popular and orchestral music.
Holiday's voice carried the weight of lived experience, reshaping how jazz vocalists interpreted song.
Parker's blazing speed and harmonic daring launched bebop, taking jazz from dance halls into the realm of art.
Monk's angular melodies and rhythmic surprises made him one of jazz's most original composers.
Davis reinvented himself across decades — cool jazz, hard bop, modal jazz, fusion — each era reshaping the genre.
Coltrane pushed jazz to its spiritual and harmonic limits, from 'sheets of sound' to the free jazz frontier.
